title: Melicope kainantuensis
taxonomy: Melicope kainantuensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It belongs to the genus Melicope, which is a group of plants native to the Pacific Islands and parts of Australia. Melicope kainantuensis is specifically found in Papua New Guinea, where it grows in the mountainous regions. The plant is a part of the order Sapindales and the class Magnoliopsida. Melicope kainantuensis is a unique species within the Rutaceae family due to its distinctive characteristics and limited geographic distribution.
uses:
I can find some information on medicinal uses of Melicope kainantuensis. 
Melicope kainantuensis is reported to have various medicinal uses, including treating fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Its leaves and stems are traditionally used by local communities to make infusions and poultices for treating these conditions. The plant is also believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and potential benefits of using Melicope kainantuensis for medicinal purposes.
